positioning: Manual-input, transparent formula/method metadata registry for Track.Lab. No AI coach, no hidden model, no fake metrics, no database dependency.

methods.limitation: Requires accurate distance and elapsed/moving time choice.
methods.sourceNote: Core arithmetic conversion used by running calculators.

methods.limitation: Output depends on distance/time accuracy and unit consistency.
Assumes pace remains constant across the full distance.
Assumes constant pace and no pauses unless user includes elapsed time.
Conversion is exact only if input pace is exact.
Conversion is exact, but source speed may be measured with error.
Requires all split distances and times to be complete and consistent.
Assumes perfectly even pacing; does not account for terrain or fatigue.

methods.formulaDisplay: secondHalfPace = targetAveragePace × adjustmentFactor; firstHalf derived from total target time
methods.limitation: Planning tool only; not a guarantee of race execution.
Interpretation depends on comparable segment length and terrain.
Requires consistent split lengths for clean interpretation.

methods.formulaDisplay: elapsedPace = elapsedTime / distance; movingPace = movingTime / distance
methods.limitation: GPS auto-pause settings can distort moving-time comparisons.
Accuracy decreases when target distance is very different from known race distance or when athlete endurance profile differs from exponent.
methods.sourceNote: Common endurance race prediction model; exponent should be user-visible and editable.

methods.formulaDisplay: relativePerformance = userResult / referenceResult × 100 or inverse depending table design
methods.limitation: Requires a valid external reference table; Track.Lab should not fabricate tables.
methods.sourceNote: Metadata placeholder only; do not implement without verified table data.

methods.methodName: Target Race Pace from Goal Time
methods.limitation: Goal may be unrealistic; this only calculates the required pace.
Assumes constant target pace.

methods.methodName: Equivalent Race Pace from Manual PB
methods.limitation: Equivalent performance is model-based and not a guarantee.
methods.sourceNote: Riegel-style prediction using manual PB input only.

methods.limitation: Flat/even split only; terrain and pacing strategy not included.
Flat/even split only; race course variation not included.
Does not include aid station time unless user manually includes it.
HRmax must be measured or manually entered; age-predicted HRmax can be inaccurate.
Requires reliable resting HR and HRmax; does not guarantee physiological zone boundaries.
LTHR must come from a valid field/lab test; heat and fatigue affect HR.
methods.sourceNote: Threshold HR zone method using user-entered LTHR.

methods.limitation: Only meaningful if HRmax and resting HR are reliable.
Requires accurate HRmax and current HR measurement.
LTHR field estimate may be inaccurate if test was poorly paced.
Not a diagnosis; affected by sleep, stress, illness, hydration, and measurement method.
Not medical diagnosis; compare only under similar effort and recovery mode.
Affected by recovery posture, heat, effort, and sensor accuracy.
Weak if pace, terrain, heat, hydration, or sensor quality changes.
Field estimate only; affected by pacing, weather, surface, motivation, and measurement accuracy.
Exact from entered distance, but distance may have measurement error.
Estimates threshold from field performance; not direct lactate measurement.
methods.sourceNote: Common field threshold pace estimation from 20-min hard effort.

methods.limitation: Requires reliable HR data and controlled 30-minute effort.
Exact from input; result quality depends on route accuracy and effort validity.
Only meaningful on comparable halves of the same route/test.
MET is an estimate and not a direct lab measurement when VO2 is estimated.
Unstable if trials are not maximal, too close in duration, or measured poorly.
D′ is model-derived and sensitive to trial quality.

methods.formulaDisplay: distance = CS × time + D′; CS = regression slope
methods.limitation: Requires at least three valid maximal trials; outliers must be visible.
Only as valid as the CS estimate.
Only valid when targetSpeed > CS; real-world fatigue is more complex.

methods.methodName: Speed from CS and D′ for Target Duration
methods.limitation: Model estimate; not a workout prescription.
Used for model fit inspection; not a performance score.
High R² does not guarantee physiological validity if trials are poor.
Factor ranges must be transparent and editable; different systems use different ranges.
methods.sourceNote: Generic deterministic zone derivation from manual race input.

methods.limitation: Field threshold pace may be inaccurate; factor model must be disclosed.
CS-derived zones depend on model quality and factor choices.
Distribution target is a planning summary, not an automatic prescription.
methods.sourceNote: Training-intensity distribution metadata; percentages must remain user-visible.

methods.limitation: Interpretation depends on zone model and accurate activity labeling.

methods.methodName: Rep Time from Distance and Pace
methods.limitation: Assumes constant rep pace.
For mixed workouts, sum each segment separately.
For mixed workouts, use sum of each segment.
Last recovery may be excluded depending workout design; UI must specify rule.
Only as accurate as entered segment durations.
Does not capture intensity or recovery quality.

methods.formulaDisplay: qualityDistance = sum(distance of segments tagged moderate/high intensity)
methods.limitation: Depends on correct segment tagging.

methods.formulaDisplay: qualityTime = sum(duration of segments tagged moderate/high intensity)
methods.limitation: Depends on correct segment tagging.
Requires target pace per segment or shared pace rule.

methods.formulaDisplay: segmentTime_i = segmentDistance_i × targetPace_i; total = Σ segmentTime_i
methods.limitation: Requires clear segment list and target pace rule.
Subjective; affected by mood, heat, sleep, and interpretation of RPE scale.
Only summarizes entered load; not injury prediction.
Can be unstable when standard deviation is very small; not a diagnosis.
Interpretation is limited; should not be used as automatic injury prediction.
Controversial as injury predictor; use only as descriptive load ratio with warnings.
methods.sourceNote: Descriptive workload ratio, not a safety guarantee.

methods.limitation: Window choice changes interpretation.
Cannot determine whether change is safe by itself.
Depends on selected zone model and accurate activity data/manual input.
Calculates observed intake only; not individualized medical nutrition advice.
Target must be user-selected; Track.Lab should not auto-prescribe aggressive intake.
Observed intake only; fluid needs are condition-specific.
Target must be user-selected; avoid overdrinking warnings required.
Observed intake only; sodium needs vary by sweat rate, diet, heat, and medical factors.
Requires total fluid > 0; product labels may be approximate.
Assumes 1 kg body mass change ≈ 1 L fluid; affected by clothing, scale, gut content, and unrecorded output.
Condition-specific; do not generalize across heat, humidity, intensity, or clothing.
Affected by scale precision and clothing water retention.
Timeline structure only; tolerance must be practiced manually.
Does not guarantee gut tolerance; product solubility varies.
Simple concentration only; not medical nutrition guidance.
Treadmill displayed speed may be miscalibrated.
Treadmill displayed speed may not equal belt speed exactly.
Estimate for steady-state running; not a lab measurement and may not fit every runner.
methods.sourceNote: ACSM metabolic equation commonly used for treadmill running estimates.

methods.limitation: Estimate for walking; less appropriate for running speeds.
methods.sourceNote: ACSM metabolic equation commonly used for treadmill walking estimates.

methods.limitation: Consumer calibration test only; not certified equipment calibration.
GPS/barometer elevation errors can be large; grade varies by segment.
Does not reflect technical terrain or descent load.
Requires validated formula selection and units; avoid hidden assumptions.
methods.sourceNote: Metadata placeholder; implement only with explicit formula and source note.

methods.limitation: Multiple approximations exist; formula must be disclosed.
methods.sourceNote: Metadata placeholder; implement only with explicit formula and source note.

methods.limitation: Complex aerodynamic model; do not implement as precise unless model is explicitly selected and validated.
Requires accurate step count and stable segment duration.

methods.methodName: Step Length from Distance and Steps
methods.limitation: Clarify step length vs stride-cycle length in UI.
Terminology varies; UI must label this clearly.

methods.methodName: Stride Length from Speed and Cadence
methods.limitation: Assumes stable speed and cadence over the measured segment.
Only valid if cadence is stable for the duration.
Requires wearable sensor data; Track.Lab manual mode should avoid fake values.
methods.sourceNote: Metadata placeholder only for manual/wearable input.

methods.limitation: Requires reliable wearable data; not a standalone form diagnosis.
methods.sourceNote: Metadata placeholder only for manual/wearable input.

methods.limitation: Descriptive only; does not score readiness automatically.
Weight fluctuates with hydration, food, and measurement conditions.
Subjective and user-defined; should not become AI readiness advice.
Requires user to assign runs manually; no automatic guarantee of shoe condition.
Economic metric only; not durability prediction.
Threshold must be user-defined; wear depends on runner, surface, shoe, and mechanics.
Very rough estimate; ignores pace, grade, economy, wind, and individual variation.
Requires real power input from device or manual entry; do not generate fake power.

methods.formulaDisplay: efficiency = speed_m_s / power_watts or power_watts / speed_m_s
methods.limitation: Device power is model-dependent and not directly comparable across brands.
Only sums entered/planned runs; not a coaching recommendation.
Only sums entered/planned runs.

methods.methodName: Long Run Share of Weekly Distance
methods.limitation: Descriptive only; does not decide whether the plan is safe.
Tagging must be manual/deterministic; no AI interpretation.
